enforced.oracle: Shuffling the order of @CO comment lines does not change the biological semantics of the SAM file; all parsed records and their fields remain identical.

evidence.quote: @CO & One-line text comment. Unordered multiple @CO lines are allowed.

enforced.oracle: Reordering @RG header lines does not change the biological semantics; each @RG line is identified by its unique ID tag, and the order among them is not significant.

evidence.quote: @RG & Read group. Unordered multiple @RG lines are allowed.

enforced.oracle: Shuffling the TAG:VALUE pairs inside the @HD header line does not change the biological semantics; the ordering of header fields within a line is not significant per the SAM v1 specification.

evidence.quote: Clarify that header field tags must be distinct within each line, and that the ordering of both header fields and alignment optional fields is not significant. (Jun 2021)

enforced.oracle: Shuffling the TAG:VALUE pairs inside each @SQ reference-sequence record does not change the biological semantics; the ordering of header fields within a line is not significant per the SAM v1 specification.

evidence.quote: Clarify that header field tags must be distinct within each line, and that the ordering of both header fields and alignment optional fields is not significant. (Jun 2021)

enforced.oracle: Shuffling the TAG:VALUE pairs inside each @RG read-group record does not change the biological semantics; the ordering of header fields within a line is not significant per the SAM v1 specification.

evidence.quote: Clarify that header field tags must be distinct within each line, and that the ordering of both header fields and alignment optional fields is not significant. (Jun 2021)

enforced.oracle: Shuffling the TAG:VALUE pairs inside each @PG program record does not change the biological semantics; the ordering of header fields within a line is not significant per the SAM v1 specification.

evidence.quote: Clarify that header field tags must be distinct within each line, and that the ordering of both header fields and alignment optional fields is not significant. (Jun 2021)

enforced.mr_name: SAM alignment optional tag field order invariance
enforced.oracle: Permuting the order of optional TAG:TYPE:VALUE fields (columns 12+) on alignment records does not change the biological semantics; the ordering of alignment optional fields is not significant per the SAM v1 specification.

evidence.quote: Clarify that header field tags must be distinct within each line, and that the ordering of both header fields and alignment optional fields is not significant. (Jun 2021)

enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))); RNEXT='=' is a synonym for RNEXT being identical to RNAME

evidence.quote: This field is set as `*' when the information is unavailable, and set as `=' if RNEXT is identical RNAME.

enforced.oracle: Every SUT must REJECT (raise validation error or crash) when the sum of query-consuming CIGAR operators (M, I, S, =, X) does not equal len(SEQ). Silent acceptance or silent record-drop is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: If not a `*', the length of the sequence must equal the sum of lengths of MIS=X operations in CIGAR.

enforced.mr_name: Query method invariance under optional tag permutation
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed query method. Shuffling optional tag fields should not change any computed properties of the alignment records.

evidence.quote: Clarify that header field tags must be distinct within each line, and that the ordering of both header fields and alignment optional fields is not significant. (Jun 2021)

enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method, where T toggles RNEXT between '=' and explicit RNAME

evidence.quote: This field is set as `*' when the information is unavailable, and set as `=' if RNEXT is identical RNAME.

enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method, where T shuffles @CO comment lines

evidence.quote: Clarify that header field tags must be distinct within each line, and that the ordering of both header fields and alignment optional fields is not significant. (Jun 2021)

quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))); SEQ field is case-insensitive per spec

evidence.quote: The case-insensitive base codes `=ACMGRSVTWYHKDBN' are mapped to [0,15] respectively

ambiguity_flags: The spec states case-insensitive mapping for BAM encoding, but SAM text format regex permits both cases

quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))); zero-length CIGAR ops contribute nothing to alignment and adjacent identical ops can be merged

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p134
evidence.quote: Adjacent CIGAR operations should be different.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: This is a Recommended Practice (SHOULD), not a MUST rule

quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))); splitting or merging adjacent identical CIGAR ops preserves total query/reference consumption

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p134
evidence.quote: Adjacent CIGAR operations should be different.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: This is a Recommended Practice (SHOULD), not a MUST rule

quarantine.oracle: Every SUT must REJECT (raise validation error or crash) when FLAG 0x4 is set on a record with RNAME != '*' and POS > 0. Silent acceptance or silent record-drop is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: Bit 0x4 is the only reliable place to tell whether the read is unmapped. If 0x4 is set, no assumptions can be made about RNAME, POS, CIGAR, MAPQ, and bits 0x2, 0x100, and 0x800.

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p137
evidence.quote: If all segments in a template are unmapped, their RNAME should be set as `*' and POS as 0.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: The spec says 'no assumptions can be made' rather than explicitly forbidding the combination, but the Recommended Practice clarifies that unmapped segments should have RNAME='*' and POS=0. The mutator creates a contradiction where 0x4 is set but RNAME/POS indicate a mapped position.

quarantine.mr_name: Optional tag illegal type character rejection
quarantine.oracle: Every SUT must REJECT (raise validation error or crash) when an optional tag's TYPE character is not one of the valid types (A, i, f, Z, H, B). Silent acceptance or silent record-drop is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: Predefined tags are described in the separate Sequence Alignment/Map Optional Fields Specification.
Predefined tags are described in the separate Sequence Alignment/Map Optional Fields Specification.

ambiguity_flags: The SAMtags spec is referenced externally; the main SAM spec does not enumerate the valid type characters directly in this chunk, but the TAG:TYPE:VALUE format is defined with TYPE being a single case-sensitive letter defining the format of VALUE.

quarantine.mr_name: POS shift with SQ LN bound preserves coordinate semantics
quarantine.oracle: After shifting all POS values by +shift and increasing @SQ LN by the same shift, the parsed alignment records' mapping positions (POS) remain valid relative to the reference length. For each record, POS + CIGAR_ref_consume <= LN must hold, and the relative ordering of records by (RNAME, POS) is preserved.

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p138
evidence.quote: [SAM v1 — Recommended Practice for the SAM Format] * If POS plus the sum of lengths of M=XDN operations in CIGAR exceeds the length specified in the LN field of the @SQ header line (if exists) with an SN equal to RNAME, the alignment should be unmapped, unless the reference sequence is circular (see below).
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

evidence.quote: [SAM v1 — The SAM Format Specification] &  LN* & Reference sequence length. Range: $[1,\,2^31-1]$

ambiguity_flags: The spec uses 'should' (advisory) rather than 'must' for the POS+LN boundary check, making this a recommended practice rather than a hard requirement

quarantine.oracle: For records with FLAG bit 0x4 (unmapped) set, MAPQ is normalized to 255. The POS field for unmapped reads should be 0 per spec recommendation, and this normalization does not change the semantic interpretation of the alignment.

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p137
evidence.quote: [SAM v1 — Recommended Practice for the SAM Format] * If all segments in a template are unmapped, their RNAME should be set as `*' and POS as 0.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p135
evidence.quote: [SAM v1 — Recommended Practice for the SAM Format] * No alignments should be assigned mapping quality 255.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: The spec uses 'should' (advisory) for unmapped POS=0, not 'must'; MAPQ 255 is also advisory

quarantine.mr_name: BAM round-trip preserves coordinate and indexing semantics
quarantine.oracle: After SAM→BAM→SAM round-trip, all alignment records preserve their POS, RNAME, and CIGAR fields such that the implied reference interval [POS, POS + CIGAR_ref_consume) is identical to the original. The @SQ LN values are unchanged.

evidence.quote: [SAM v1 — Indexing BAM] Indexing aims to achieve fast retrieval of alignments overlapping a specified region without going through the whole alignments. BAM must be sorted by the reference ID and then the leftmost coordinate before indexing.
[SAM v1 — The SAM Format Specification] The alignment section: mandatory fields... Each line consists of 11~or more TAB-separated fields.

ambiguity_flags: BAM encoding may canonicalize certain CIGAR representations (e.g., collapsing =/X to M) which could change the CIGAR string while preserving the reference interval

quarantine.mr_name: SAM SEQ case normalization invariance with query methods
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method, where T uppercases SEQ field

evidence.quote: Each alignment line has 11 mandatory fields for essential alignment information such as mapping position, and variable number of optional fields for flexible or aligner specific information.

ambiguity_flags: SAMv1 §1.4.10 says 'No assumptions can be made on the letter cases' for SEQ, meaning case is not semantically significant
